topic: An artist's work is displayed without their permission
raw_output:
[story 1]

The gallery lights felt like physical spotlights, burning directly into my exposed skin. I stood near a canvas that was undeniably mine—a swirling landscape I’d poured months of my soul into—but now it was surrounded by strangers, their murmurs a low, invasive hum. Every time someone paused, their eyes drifting to my work, I felt a tightening in my chest, a sudden, desperate need to melt into the polished floor. My hands, usually steady when mixing paint, were trembling slightly as I watched a critic lean in, nodding slowly. I wanted to vanish, to become just another shadow in the corner, anything but the focal point of this unexpected attention.

[story 2]

"It’s quite striking," a voice drifted over. I gripped the edge of my easel, my knuckles whitening beneath the thin fabric of my smock. I could feel the heat rising in my cheeks, a flush that had nothing to do with the gallery temperature. I kept my gaze fixed on a distant spot on the ceiling, refusing to meet anyone’s eyes. Each appreciative glance felt like a probe, assessing, judging the worth of what I had created. I fiddled incessantly with a loose thread on my cuff, my breath catching in shallow gasps, wishing the floor would simply swallow me whole.

[story 3]

I watched from across the room as a young man pointed, a wide, eager smile spreading across his face. A wave of intense awkwardness washed over me, making my throat feel suddenly dry. I tried to take a slow, deep breath, but it caught in my lungs. I shifted my weight from one foot to the other, a nervous tic I couldn't suppress. The silence after his comment was deafening, amplifying the sound of my own frantic heartbeat. I wanted to retreat behind the protective barrier of my own solitude, to disappear into the anonymity of the crowd, leaving the artwork, and by extension, myself, untouched by this overwhelming scrutiny.
